differentials.name: Well-differentiated liposarcoma
differentials.raw: Well-differentiated liposarcoma (no distinguishing imaging features, patient age < 3 years allows reliable differentiation)

context: =  rare rapidly growing benign tumor in children < 3 years of age (= postnatal proliferation) composed of mesenchymal cells ranging from prelipoblasts (spindle cells) to mature adipocytes Origin:   derived from fetal adipose tissue Frequency:   30% of adipocytic tumors in children Path:   encapsulated immature adipose tissue (embryonal fat) separated by septa into multiple lobules Histo:   uni- and multivacuolated lipoblasts interspersed between spindle / stellate mesenchymal cells + suspended in myxoid stroma Molecular features:   translocation involving 8q11-13 with rearrangement of Plag1 ge
